title: Kafka Consumers Deep Dive
learningObjectives: Explain **poll** semantics and how **max.poll.interval** ties to handler runtime
Compare **auto**, **sync**, and **async** commits with failure scenarios
Use **pause**/**resume** and **rebalance** listeners for production-safe consumers
Contrast **static** membership and cooperative rebalance trade-offs

sections.title: Why Kafka Consumers Deep Dive matters
sections.content:
**Consumer** tuning is where **SLOs** meet **reality**: **poll** cadence, **commit** points, and **rebalance** behavior decide whether you ship **duplicates**, **loss**, or **lag**. Interviewers love **max.poll.interval** war stories.

This day pairs with **producer** durability: **end-to-end** thinking separates **senior** answers from **tutorial** answers.

sections.title: Theory and Internals - Kafka Consumers Deep Dive
sections.content:
### Day 61 — **Kafka** **consumers**

### 1. Poll loop
**poll** balances **fetch**, **heartbeat**, and **rebalance** participation.

### 2. Offset commit modes
**Auto** vs **sync** manual vs **async** manual—each trades **latency**, **throughput**, and **failure** semantics.

### 3. Rebalance
**Partition** **revocation** **stops** **processing**—design **idempotent** **handlers**.

### 4. Backpressure
**pause**/**resume** and **downstream** **queue** depths.

### 5. Isolation level
Interacts with **transactional** **producers**.

### 6. Story
**Auto** commit + DB write reorder → **finance** mismatch; **manual** commit after **persist**.

sections.title: Basic — Consumer vocabulary
sections.output:
poll: fetch + heartbeat driver
commit: persist group offset cursor
rebalance: partition ownership moves

sections.title: Intermediate — Commit after process simulation
sections.description: Toy state machine: processed flag gates commit.

sections.title: Advanced — Per-partition lag toy
Consumer group coordinator
sections.plantuml:
@startuml
title Day 61 — Consumer group
participant Coordinator
participant Consumer
Coordinator -> Consumer : assignment
loop poll
  Consumer -> Coordinator : heartbeat + fetch
end
@enduml

sections.title: Exercise — Kafka consumers — offset commit modes (Day 61 assignment)
sections.problem: Print **exactly** the assignment line on offset commit trade-offs.
hints: One println, match assignments JSON verbatim.

sections.title: Interview Drill

conceptual.question: What is **poll** loop responsibility?
conceptual.answer: **poll** **fetches** records, **drives** **partition** **fetch** **negotiation**, and **sends** **heartbeats**; **long** gaps **trigger** **rebalance**. Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Kafka consumers**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. **poll** **fetches** records, **drives** **partition** **fetch** **negotiation**, and **sends** **he Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail. Close with **prevention**: **contract** tests for **clients**, **SLO** alerts on **client-side** **error** rates, **game** days that **kill** **pods** during **business** hours in **staging**, and **lint** rules banning **blocking** calls on **reactor** threads.

followUps.question: How do you **roll out** a policy change here without a **retry** **storm**?
followUps.answer: Use **feature** **flags**, **canary** **deployments**, **tight** **retry** **budgets** on **non-idempotent** calls, and **verify** with **load** tests that **match** **production** **fan-out**. **poll** **fetches** records, **drives** **partition** **fetch** **negotiation**, and **se Tie answers to **Spring** reality: **Feign** + **LoadBalancer**, **WebClient** + **`ExchangeFilterFunction`**, **Resilience4j** registries, and **Micrometer** tags that make **on-call** searches fast. Explain **second-order** effects: **retry** amplification, **queue** growth, **thread** **pool** exhaustion, and **cost** of extra **hops** when **edge** dependencies like **Redis** rate-limit stores fail.

conceptual.question: **enable.auto.commit** behavior?
conceptual.answer: Consumer **periodically** **commits** **offsets** **without** your **code** **choosing** **exactly** **after** **success** **processing**. Call out **ownership**: **platform** **SRE** for **mesh**/**certs**, **service** teams for **timeout** policy, **SRE** for **SLO** **burn** **alerts**—and how those teams **coordinate** during **sev-1** calls. Interviewers reward naming **metrics** (`http_client_requests_seconds`, `resilience4j_circuitbreaker_state`), **trace** correlation IDs, and **rollback** criteria rather than hand-wavy resilience claims.

followUps.question: Production follow-up: which **dashboards** or **metrics** expose this for **Kafka consumers**?
followUps.answer: Start with **client** **outbound** **histograms** and **resilience4j** **circuitbreaker** **state** **gauges**, then open **distributed** **traces** filtered by **service** **name** and **route**. Consumer **periodically** **commits** **offsets** **without** your **code** **choosing** **exactly** For **gateway** and **service** stacks, emphasize **timeout** ordering (**outer > inner**), **bulkheads** per **tenant**, and **JWT** **cache** **TTL** during **issuer** rotations. Communicate **executive**-friendly **impact**: **customer** **minutes** degraded, **error** **budget** burn, **mitigation** **ETA**, and **post-incident** **themes** mapped to **roadmap** work.

Reference **security**: **mTLS** **service** identity, **OAuth2** **scopes**, **audit** logs at **edge**, and why **secrets** never belong in **Feign** **URL** query strings. Contrast **happy** **path** docs with **partition** behavior: **split** **retries**, **half-open** **probes**, **degraded** **read** modes, and **idempotency** keys that prevent **duplicate** **writes**.

**(2) Root cause** Sequential **processing** **too** **slow**.

**(3) Fix** **Delegate** per partition or **add** **instances** to **match** **partition** **count**.

**(4) Prevention** Profile **poll** **loop**. wrongAnswers: **Auto** commit is always safe — **Correction:** Can **lose** or **duplicate** relative to **processing**.
**Consumer** can **read** **uncommitted** DB and be fine — **Correction:** **Split** **brain** vs **offset**.
**Seek** fixes **broker** lag — **Correction:** **Seek** only **client** cursor; fix **throughput** separately.

sections.title: Cheatsheet
sections.content:
| Topic | Recall |
|---|---|
| poll | Fetch + heartbeat |
| auto.commit | Easy but fuzzy commit point |
| commitSync | Stronger control, blocks |
| rebalance | Partition moves |
| max.poll.interval | Must exceed handler |
| lag | highWater - position |
| pause | Client backpressure |
| isolation | read_committed with tx |
| static membership | Fewer surprise rebalances |
| deserializer errors | DLQ / skip policy |
